summary: The June 30 selection emphasizes heavy-ion phenomenology for spin-alignment systematics, p-Pb heavy-flavor collectivity, high-density nuclear equation-of-state constraints, quarkonium perturbative baselines, and physics-aware ML methods for nuclear structure, HEP density morphing, PDE emulation, and detector-device simulation.
strongest_paper_summary: The K*0 rescattering paper is the most directly actionable heavy-ion study because it identifies a concrete mechanism by which hadronic rescattering can generate apparent vector-meson spin-alignment signals even without intrinsic polarization.

papers.title: Nuclear equation-of-state at high density and multi-messenger astronomy: contribution of heavy-ion collisions
papers.abstract: Review of how intermediate-energy heavy-ion collision observables, especially flow, constrain the high-density nuclear equation of state and connect to neutron-star pressure constraints from multi-messenger astronomy.

papers.title: Anisotropic hadronic rescattering and its impact on K*0 yield, and polarization observable
papers.abstract: AMPT study showing that anisotropic daughter rescattering can distort reconstructed K*0 yields and generate apparent rho00 spin-alignment deviations even for unpolarized input samples.

papers.title: Investigating forward-backward asymmetry in D-meson production and anisotropic flow in p-Pb collisions at the LHC
papers.authors_short: Siyu Tang; Chao Zhang; Liang Zheng; Renzhuo Wan; Zi-Wei Lin; Guo-Liang Ma
papers.abstract: Heavy-flavor-improved string-melting AMPT study of prompt D0 RpPb and v2 forward-backward asymmetries in p-Pb collisions at 8.16 TeV.

papers.title: Soft Contributions Stabilize NNLO QCD Corrections to Quarkonium Production and Decay
papers.authors_short: Luca Maxia; Hua-Sheng Shao; Lukas Simon; Guoxing Wang
papers.abstract: Identifies the soft-contribution origin of perturbative instabilities in NNLO NRQCD quarkonium production and decay and proposes a stabilization method for S-wave color-singlet processes.

papers.title: Revisiting identified-particle pT spectra using the Boltzmann-Gibbs blast-wave model in a Bayesian inference framework
papers.authors_short: Z. Xie; W.Z. Li; J.Q. Tao; H. Zheng; W.C. Zhang; and 5 others
papers.abstract: Bayesian BGBW analysis of pion, kaon, and proton pT spectra in Au+Au and Pb+Pb, emphasizing simultaneous fits without species-dependent pT ranges and posterior correlations for kinetic freeze-out parameters.

papers.title: Construction of Nuclear Covariant Energy Density Functional from A Physics-Guaranteed Neural Network Approach
papers.authors_short: W. F. Li; Z. M. Niu; H. Z. Liang; Y. F. Niu; B. H. Sun
papers.abstract: Physics-guaranteed neural-network construction of a nuclear covariant energy density functional, reporting improved binding-energy accuracy and extrapolation behavior relative to traditional ML approaches.

papers.title: Factorizable Normalizing Flows for parameter-dependent density morphing
papers.authors_short: Davide Valsecchi; Mauro Donega; Rainer Wallny
papers.abstract: Normalizing-flow framework for parameter-dependent density morphing, learning factorized polynomial transformations around a reference density for scalable unbinned likelihood modeling in HEP.

papers.title: Randomized neural operator for parametric PDEs with fast training and conformal uncertainty quantification
papers.authors_short: Zirui Deng; Jingbo Sun; Deyu Meng; Fei Wang
papers.abstract: PCA-RaNN combines PCA latent compression, fixed random features, closed-form readout, ensemble averaging, and split-conformal intervals for fast parametric PDE operator learning.

papers.title: PCGD: Physics-Guided Conditional Graph Diffusion for TCAD Device Simulation
papers.authors_short: Yihan Zhang; Zhiteng Zhang; Kun Chen; Chen Wang
papers.abstract: Physics-guided graph diffusion surrogate for TCAD semiconductor device simulations on unstructured meshes, using boundary-condition conditioning and PDE-residual objectives.
